system.name: Reproductive System
system.shortDescription: Female: ovaries, uterus, fallopian tubes, vagina, breast tissue. Male: testes, prostate, seminal vesicles, penis. The system of regeneration, fertility, and intimate function.
system.elementalProfile: Water (Scorpio depth) + Fire (Mars drive) + Earth (Taurus pleasure body)

anatomy.vertebralCorrelates: Per Cornell: L1-L5 + sacrum innervates pelvic organs. T10-T12 for ovaries/testes. Bodyworkers: sacral mobilization + L5/S1 freedom supports pelvic function.
anatomy.nervePlexus: Hypogastric plexus (sympathetic), pelvic splanchnic nerves (parasympathetic — erection, vaginal lubrication, uterine), pudendal nerve.

astrologicalRulership.primaryPlanet: Mars (male) / Venus (female), Pluto (deep reproductive transformation), Moon (cyclical)
astrologicalRulership.primaryPlanetRationale: Scorpio is the primary sign for reproductive — both Mars (traditional) and Pluto (modern) rule. Cornell (1933): 'Scorpio rules the sexual functions, the reproductive organs.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Male reproductive primary, external genitals both sexes, libido, testosterone
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Mars rules the external genitals and the male reproductive function.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Female reproductive primary, ovaries, internal female organs, estrogen, breasts (with Moon)
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Venus rules the female generative organs.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Uterus, menstrual cycle, breast tissue, motherhood/nurturing, fertility cycles
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'The Moon rules the female cycle, the breasts, the uterus.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Deep transformation of reproductive function — menopause, andropause, fertility/infertility, hormonal restructuring
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Modern: Pluto = deep transformation; Pluto rules Scorpio = reproductive

secondaryPlanets.rules: Reproductive aging, menopause structural changes, low libido (chronic), prostate enlargement
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Saturn restricts; reproductive function may decline.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Reproductive expansion, pregnancy/gestation, growth of fetus, multiple births
Idiopathic reproductive symptoms, unexplained infertility, fibrocystic patterns
Sudden reproductive events, irregular cycles, unexpected pregnancy

axes.name: Regeneration / Reproductive Axis
axes.interpretation: THE primary reproductive axis. Taurus (Venus pleasure body, hormonal physical) opposite Scorpio (Pluto deep regenerative, transformation through sexual energy). This axis governs the entire arc from pleasure → fertility → transformation.

energetics.chakra: Sacral (Svadhisthana) primary — sexuality, creativity, pleasure. Root (Muladhara) for grounding sexuality. Heart for intimacy.
energetics.nervousSystemPathway: Pelvic splanchnic parasympathetic (engorgement, lubrication, uterine), hypogastric sympathetic (orgasm), pudendal sensory
energetics.endocrineCorrespondence: HPG axis (hypothalamus → pituitary → gonads). Estrogen, progesterone, testosterone, FSH, LH, prolactin, oxytocin
energetics.meridianCrossover_TCM: Kidney meridian (essence/jing — reproductive substance). Ren Mai (Conception Vessel) — directly named for this function. Chong Mai (Penetrating Vessel) — Sea of Blood.

cellSalts.indicatedFor: Uterine prolapse, vaginal laxity (postpartum), hemorrhoid/varicose patterns, fibrotic changes.
Heavy menstrual bleeding → anemia, postpartum iron support.

sacredBotanicals.name: Damiana
sacredBotanicals.mechanism: Reproductive aphrodisiac; supports both male and female

supplementaryHerbs.name: Vitex (chasteberry)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: PMS, irregular cycles, mild infertility — perimenopause + reproductive years

supplementaryHerbs.name: Black cohosh
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Menopausal hot flashes. AVOID with liver disease.

supplementaryHerbs.name: Maca
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Both male and female hormonal balance, fertility, libido

supplementaryHerbs.name: Saw palmetto
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: BPH (benign prostate enlargement), male hair loss

supplementaryHerbs.name: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Female fertility, menstrual irregularity, menopause, lactation

supplementaryHerbs.name: Dong quai (Angelica sinensis)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Menstrual irregularity, blood deficiency patterns (TCM framework)

bodyProtocols.breath: Pelvic floor breath (down on inhale, up on exhale)

modalityRecommendations.individual: Reproductive health responds to: cycle awareness (track if menstruating), hormone-supportive nutrition, stress management (cortisol disrupts sex hormones), sleep, pleasure as medicine. Address concerns with gynecologist/urologist as appropriate. Sexual function concerns benefit from sex-positive therapy + medical evaluation if persistent.
modalityRecommendations.medical_astrologer: Scorpio + 8th house primary. Venus (female) / Mars (male). Moon for cycle. Pluto for deep transformations (menopause, fertility journeys, sexual healing). Outer planet transits to Venus/Mars/Moon often mark significant reproductive events.
modalityRecommendations.reiki: Sacral chakra PRIMARY extended (sometimes whole session). Trauma-informed approach essential — sexual energy work requires consent + safety. Distance reiki often preferred. Pelvic bowl meditation.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_general: Pelvic floor (SPECIALIZED training required). Visceral pelvic work (advanced — Barral). Maya abdominal massage. Sacral mobilization. Always trauma-informed for this region.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_contraindications: Pregnancy: TRAINED prenatal/postpartum massage only. Sexual trauma history: explicit consent + ongoing communication. Active PID, recent surgery, IUD insertion < 6 weeks: caution. Cancer: oncology massage cert.
modalityRecommendations.naturopathy: Hormone testing (cycle-timed for women). Vitex, maca, shatavari (female). Saw palmetto, tribulus (male). Address gut (estrogen metabolism). Manage stress (cortisol steals from progesterone — 'pregnenolone steal'). Vitamin D, zinc, omega-3.
modalityRecommendations.acupuncture: Conception Vessel (Ren Mai). Kidney meridian (essence). Spleen 6 (Sanyinjiao — three yin crossing, female reproductive master point). LV 3, SP 6, KD 3 — classical reproductive trio.
modalityRecommendations.ayurveda_phase2_placeholder: Shukra Dhatu (reproductive — 7th dhatu, ultimate essence). Apana Vata (downward — menstruation, ejaculation). Shatavari, ashoka, ashwagandha. Phase 2.
modalityRecommendations.pastoral_spiritual: Sacred sexuality. Body as temple. Sexual energy as creative energy. Trauma-informed sexual healing work. Yoni/lingam reverence practices.

pathologies.name: PMS / PMDD
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Moon afflictions → menstrual irregularity, premenstrual distress.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: PMS symptoms benefit from: vitex (chasteberry), magnesium, B6, calcium, omega-3, regular exercise, stress reduction, blood sugar stability. Severe PMS or PMDD (with significant mood disruption) benefits from medical evaluation — many effective interventions available.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Moon-Venus-Mars configurations classically associated with menstrual cyclic patterns (Cornell, 1933). Vitex evidence-supported for PMS. PMDD is a more serious clinical picture — refer to psychiatry/gyn for SSRI consideration in severe cases.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Pre-menstrual: pelvic congestion work, lower back release
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Vitex, magnesium, B6, omega-3, exercise, stress reduction

redFlags: Severe mood disturbance with suicidal ideation → urgent mental health

pathologies.name: Endometriosis
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Chronic pelvic congestion + inflammation patterns with malefic afflictions in 8th house.' Modern endometriosis recognized as estrogen-driven inflammatory condition.

indicatorLanguage.individual: Endometriosis is a chronic condition requiring proper medical evaluation (laparoscopy is the gold standard) and often specialty care. Severe period pain that disrupts your life is not normal. Pelvic pain specialist or endometriosis-knowledgeable gynecologist makes a significant difference.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Pluto-Moon and Mars-Saturn reproductive configurations align. Endometriosis is gynecology/pelvic pain specialty. Holistic adjuncts: anti-inflammatory diet, address gut-estrogen axis (DIM, calcium D-glucarate), magnesium, omega-3, pelvic floor PT, stress management. Surgical excision (not ablation) for moderate-severe.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Pelvic floor PT (specialty), visceral mobilization (specialty), gentle abdominal work. Avoid during flares.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Anti-inflammatory diet, gluten/dairy trial, gut health, omega-3, pelvic floor PT

redFlags: Severe acute pelvic pain → ED (rule out cyst rupture, torsion)

pathologies.name: Infertility (general)
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Sterility — Saturn afflictions in 5th house or to reproductive significators.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Fertility journeys are deeply personal. Standard workup at 12 months trying (6 months if > 35yo): hormone panel, semen analysis, imaging. Lifestyle support is real and meaningful: nutrition, weight optimization, stress management, environmental toxin reduction. Reproductive endocrinology offers many options.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn in 5th or to reproductive significators classically associated with fertility challenges (Cornell, 1933). Both partners need workup. Refer to reproductive endocrinology if appropriate. Holistic adjuncts: comprehensive nutrition (CoQ10, vitamin D, methylated folate, omega-3), stress management, acupuncture (evidence-supported), maca, shatavari (women), tribulus (men).

modalityNotes.bodywork: Maya abdominal massage (specialty), fertility-focused massage protocols
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Comprehensive nutrition, lifestyle, environmental factors, stress management

redFlags: Sudden severe pelvic pain in pregnancy (ectopic) → 911

pathologies.name: BPH (Benign Prostate Enlargement)
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Prostatic enlargement — Saturn-Jupiter combination; chronic male organ change with age.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Common in men over 50. Symptoms (slow stream, dribbling, nighttime urination) often respond to: saw palmetto, pygeum, zinc, lifestyle. Sudden urinary retention or blood in urine requires medical evaluation. Annual prostate health discussion with healthcare provider after 50 (or 45 if African-American or family history).
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-Jupiter male reproductive configurations classically associated with prostatic enlargement (Cornell, 1933). Saw palmetto, pygeum, beta-sitosterol, zinc, pumpkin seed evidence-supported. Refer urology for medication or procedural consideration in severe symptoms. PSA + DRE annually.

modalityNotes.lifestyle: Saw palmetto 320mg/day, zinc, lycopene, reduce alcohol, regular ejaculation may be protective

redFlags: Blood in urine → urology workup (rule out cancer)

pathologies.name: Menopause Transition
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Climacteric — outer planet transits to Moon, profound feminine transformation.'
cellSalts: Calc Fluor (tissue), Kali Phos (nervous), Nat Mur (emotional)

indicatorLanguage.individual: Menopause is a transition, not a disease. Symptoms vary widely. Many options exist: lifestyle (movement, diet, stress, sleep), herbs (black cohosh, sage, maca), bioidentical hormone therapy (discuss with menopause-trained provider). Post-menopause bone density (DEXA) and cardiovascular monitoring important.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Outer planet transits to Moon/Venus mark menopausal timing. Symptom-specific protocols: vasomotor (black cohosh, sage, BHRT), GU syndrome (vaginal estrogen, ospemifene), mood (consider SSRI vs SNRI, HRT), sleep, bone, cardio. Refer to NAMS-certified menopause specialist for comprehensive care.

modalityNotes.lifestyle: Strength training, calcium/D/K2, sleep, stress, social connection, BHRT consideration

complianceNotes.scopeOfPractice: Reproductive issues are intimate and often require specialty medical care (gyn, urology, repro endocrinology). Pregnancy is its own scope. Sexual trauma requires trauma-informed care.
criticalReferralRules: Sudden severe pelvic pain → ED (ovarian torsion, cyst rupture, ectopic)
Blood in urine male over 50 → urology workup
